zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Willoughby VI於1310年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛時，他的父親Richard de Willoughby V51歲，母親Elizabeth de Vautort41歲。他與Emma Mortein結婚。他於1332年被列為lord chief justice。他去世於1362年3月在英國英格蘭素瑞可洛頓享年52歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲。

nameMeanings.name: Willoughby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Willoughby, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Nottinghamshire, and Warwickshire. They are named from an Old English +wilig ‘willow’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.

variants.name: Wilby
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Wilby, in Suffolk, Norfolk, and Northamptonshire. The first placename is probably named from an Old English wilig ‘willow’ + Old English bēag ‘circle, ring’; the Norfolk placename derives from Old English wilig + bēag or Old Norse bý ‘farmstead, village’; the Northamptonshire placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Vili + bý.
Redmonds has demonstrated that in the parish of Hipperholme, and subsequently elsewhere in Yorkshire, Wilby occurs as a variant of Willoughby .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Willoughby VI was born in 1310, in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England, his father, Richard de Willoughby V, was 51 and his mother, Elizabeth de Vautort, was 41. He married Emma Mortein. In 1332, his occupation is listed as lord chief justice. He died in March 1362, in Croydon, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Willoughby VI est né en 1310, à Loughborough, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Willoughby V, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Vautort, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Emma Mortein. En 1332, sa profession de son indiquée est lord chief justice. Il est décédé en mars 1362, à Croydon, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

Er hat Emma Mortein geheiratet. 1332 ist sein Beruf als lord chief justice aufgeführt. ja.lifeSummary: Richard Willoughby VIが1310年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアロウボロウで生まれた時，父Richard de Willoughby Vは51歳，母Elizabeth de Vautortは41歳であった。Emma Morteinと結婚した。1332年，職業はlord chief justiceと記録されている。彼は1362年3月にイギリスイギリススリークロイドンで52歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアウィロウビー オン ス ウオルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Willoughby VI(이)가 1310년 영국 레이스스터실르 루그h버루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Willoughby V(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Vautort(은)는 41세였다. 그는 Emma Mortein(와)과 결혼했다. 1332년, 그의 직업은 lord chief justice이었다. 그는 1362년 3월 영국 잉글랜드 수리 크로이던에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 윌루그h비  테 울드즈에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Willoughby VI родился в 1310 г. в местности лоугбороую, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Willoughby V было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Vautort, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Emma Mortein. В 1332 году он работал по профессии lord chief justice. Он умер марта 1362 г. в местности кроидон, сурри, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности уиллоугби он т волдс, ноттингхамшир, Англия.
